patch.value: Workspace singleton files (settings.json, schema, session-state, checkpoints) are lock-free read-modify-write — parallel pm config set demonstrably loses updates

`pm config set` and other singleton-file writers do read→modify→writeFileAtomic with NO lock, so concurrent writers last-writer-win and silently drop each other's changes. Empirically demonstrated: looped two parallel `pm config set` on DIFFERENT keys (author_default vs locks_ttl_seconds) → the locks_ttl_seconds write was lost in 3 of 6 rounds (value stuck at stale round's value while the other key advanced). Affected writers (all read-modify-write without acquireLock): src/core/store/settings.ts (~L1313, pm config set / governance preset), src/core/schema/runtime-schema.ts (~L588, pm schema add-type/add-field/add-status), src/core/session/session-state.ts (~L56, pm focus / focus inheritance), src/core/checkpoint/mutation-checkpoint.ts (~L119). Fix: route these through the existing lock infra with reserved workspace-scoped lock ids (e.g. __settings, __schema, __session) held across the read-modify-write window — same TTL/stale-sweep semantics as item locks, and pm gc --scope locks sweeps debris for free. Atomicity of the write itself is fine (temp+rename); the lost-update window is the read→write gap.
settings.json, schema, session-state, and checkpoint writes use singleton-lock discipline; parallel pm config set loses zero updates under stress.

## Context
Filed by the 2026-07-03 concurrency audit. All workspace singleton files are written read→modify→writeFileAtomic with NO lock, so parallel writers silently last-writer-win.

## Empirical repro
Two parallel `pm config set` loops on DIFFERENT keys (author_default vs locks_ttl_seconds): the locks_ttl_seconds write was lost in 3 of 6 rounds — the file kept one writer's view, dropping the other's committed change with exit 0.

## Affected writers (all read-modify-write without acquireLock)
- src/core/store/settings.ts (~L1313) — pm config set / governance preset
- src/core/schema/runtime-schema.ts (~L588) — pm schema add-type/add-field/add-status
- src/core/session/session-state.ts (~L56) — pm focus
- checkpoints state writers

## Approach
Reuse the existing per-item lock machinery with a reserved singleton lock id per file (e.g. __settings__, __schema__), wrapping read+modify+write. After pm-2muu lands, contended singleton writes get the same bounded-wait self-healing instead of failing. Alternative (compare-and-swap on content hash with one retry) is weaker — still loses under sustained contention.

## Definition of done
Parallel config/schema/focus write stress shows zero lost updates; lock is released on error paths; docs note the singleton lock ids; evidence recorded on this item.
